title: agrostis cornucopiae
intro: Agrostis cornucopiae, also known as the Fescue Grass or Cock's-foot, is a perennial grass species native to Europe and Asia. Traditionally, Agrostis cornucopiae has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The plant's medicinal properties are believed to provide relief from various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory issues, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Agrostis cornucopiae can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various herbal preparations.
